feed.title: Pronounce — recent dictionary additions
feed.subtitle: How engineers actually pronounce project / product / jargon names. Community-maintained.

entry.title: avif — ay vif
entry.summary: avif is pronounced "ay vif" (/ˈeɪvɪf/). AV1 Image Format; "AY-vif".

entry.title: heic — aitch ee eye see
entry.summary: heic is pronounced "aitch ee eye see" (/ˌeɪtʃ iː aɪ ˈsiː/). Apple High-Efficiency image; spelled "H-E-I-C", some say "hake".

entry.title: webp — web pee
entry.summary: webp is pronounced "web pee" (/ˌwɛb ˈpiː/). Google image format; "web-P".

entry.title: flac — flak
entry.summary: flac is pronounced "flak" (/flæk/). Free Lossless Audio Codec; "flak".

entry.title: ogg — og
entry.summary: ogg is pronounced "og" (/ɒɡ/). Ogg audio container; "og".

entry.title: mkv — em kay vee
entry.summary: mkv is pronounced "em kay vee" (/ˌɛm keɪ ˈviː/). Matroska video container; letters "M-K-V".

entry.title: svg — ess vee jee
entry.summary: svg is pronounced "ess vee jee" (/ˌɛs viː ˈdʒiː/). Scalable Vector Graphics; letters "S-V-G".

entry.title: jpeg — jay peg
entry.summary: jpeg is pronounced "jay peg" (/ˈdʒeɪpɛɡ/). Joint Photographic Experts Group; "JAY-peg".

entry.title: ico — eye koh
entry.summary: ico is pronounced "eye koh" (/ˈaɪkoʊ/). Windows icon format; "EYE-koh" or letters "I-C-O".

entry.title: woff — woff
entry.summary: woff is pronounced "woff" (/wɒf/). Web Open Font Format; "woff".

entry.title: facade — fuh sahd
entry.summary: facade is pronounced "fuh sahd" (/fəˈsɑːd/). Design pattern; "fuh-SAHD" (the ç/c is soft).

entry.title: paradigm — pa ruh dime
entry.summary: paradigm is pronounced "pa ruh dime" (/ˈpærədaɪm/). "PA-ruh-dime"; the g is silent.

entry.title: heuristic — hyoo riss tik
entry.summary: heuristic is pronounced "hyoo riss tik" (/hjʊˈrɪstɪk/). "hyoo-RISS-tik".

entry.title: mnemonic — nih mon ik
entry.summary: mnemonic is pronounced "nih mon ik" (/nɪˈmɒnɪk/). "nih-MON-ik"; the m is silent.

entry.title: ephemeral — ih fem er ul
entry.summary: ephemeral is pronounced "ih fem er ul" (/ɪˈfɛmərəl/). Short-lived (ephemeral storage/ports); "ih-FEM-er-ul".

entry.title: pseudo — soo doh
entry.summary: pseudo is pronounced "soo doh" (/ˈsuːdoʊ/). "SOO-doh"; the p is silent. Not the same as `sudo`.

entry.title: quasi — kway zye
entry.summary: quasi is pronounced "kway zye" (/ˈkweɪzaɪ/). "KWAY-zye" (US) vs "KWAH-zee".

entry.title: verbose — ver bohss
entry.summary: verbose is pronounced "ver bohss" (/vɜːrˈboʊs/). As in --verbose; "ver-BOHSS".

entry.title: pycharm — pie charm
entry.summary: pycharm is pronounced "pie charm" (/ˈpaɪtʃɑːrm/). JetBrains Python IDE; "PIE-charm".

entry.title: webstorm — web storm
entry.summary: webstorm is pronounced "web storm" (/ˈwɛbstɔːrm/). JetBrains JS IDE; "web-storm".

entry.title: spring — spring
entry.summary: spring is pronounced "spring" (/sprɪŋ/). Java framework; "spring", like the season.

entry.title: opengl — open jee ell
entry.summary: opengl is pronounced "open jee ell" (/ˌoʊpən dʒiː ˈɛl/). Graphics API; "open-G-L".

entry.title: directx — dih rekt eks
entry.summary: directx is pronounced "dih rekt eks" (/dɪˈrɛkt ɛks/). Microsoft graphics API; "Direct-X".

entry.title: metal — met ul
entry.summary: metal is pronounced "met ul" (/ˈmɛtəl/). Apple graphics API; "metal", like the material.

entry.title: threejs — three jay ess
entry.summary: threejs is pronounced "three jay ess" (/ˌθriː dʒeɪ ˈɛs/). Three.js WebGL library; "three-J-S".
